input.text: Create a new coordinate system by setting the Euler angles to negative ninety degrees, zero degrees, and negative ninety degrees. Set the translation vector to zero, zero point zero five six one, and zero. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In the first loop, draw the first line from the coordinates zero, zero point five seven two four to zero point zero seven two three, zero point five seven two four. Draw the first arc from zero point zero seven two three, zero point five seven two four to zero point five six five four, zero point five seven two four, with a midpoint at zero point three one eight nine, zero point zero five nine two. Draw the second line from zero point five six five four, zero point five seven two four to zero point six three seven seven, zero point five seven two four. Draw the second arc from zero point six three seven seven, zero point five seven two four to zero, zero point five seven two four, with a midpoint at zero point three one eight nine, zero. Scale the sketch using a scaling factor of zero point six three seven seven. Rotate the scaled sketch using the Euler angles of negative ninety degrees, zero degrees, and negative ninety degrees. Translate the sketch using the translation vector of zero, zero point zero five six one, and zero. Extrude the sketch zero point five five two six units in the direction of the normal, ensuring no extrusion in the opposite direction. Create a new solid body using the extruded shape. The final dimensions of the U-shaped object are length zero point six three seven seven one six nine four nine eight five four nine five two three units, width zero point five seven two three six eight four two one zero five two six three one five units, and height zero point five five two six three one five seven eight nine four seven three six eight five units.

Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero and a translation vector also set to zero. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face. Within this face, draw a single loop and within this loop, draw a circle centered at coordinates [0.375, 0.375] with a radius of 0.375 units. Apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the two-dimensional sketch. Rotate the scaled sketch using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translate it using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Extrude the sketch along the normal direction by 0.2222 units to create a three-dimensional model. Ensure that this extrusion forms a new solid body. The final dimensions of the cylindrical object are: length of 0.7500000148612537 units, width of 0.7500000148612537 units, and height of 0.2221685537092858 units.

Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector also set to zero. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face, starting with the first line from the point (0.1875, 0.0) to (0.5625, 0.0). Follow this with the first arc, which starts at (0.5625, 0.0), passes through the midpoint (0.75, 0.1875), and ends at (0.5625, 0.375). Continue with the second line from (0.5625, 0.375) to (0.1875, 0.375). Complete the loop with the second arc, starting at (0.1875, 0.375), passing through (0.0, 0.1875), and ending back at (0.1875, 0.0). Scale the entire sketch by a factor of 0.75. Transform the scaled sketch into three dimensions by rotating it with Euler angles set to zero and translating it with a vector of zero. Extrude the sketch along the normal direction by 0.15 units to create a solid body. The resulting shape is a three-dimensional object with a cylindrical base and an elliptical top, measuring 0.75 units in length, 0.375 units in width, and 0.15 units in height.
